title: How XDC and SDC constraints model timing
description: Constraint languages declare clocks, input and output delays, and exceptions that define the timing problem STA solves.
body: Use when writing or reading timing constraints. Do not treat constraints as suggestions, since they define the entire timing problem the tool optimizes against. Actions: declare clocks first, add input and output delays for I/O, and apply exceptions only where semantics justify. Validation signals include a constraint set that fully defines the timing problem and an STA run with no missing-constraint warnings. Risk boundary: a wrong delay value skews every related path, so flag unverified I/O delays as a source of false slack.
